stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 3 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability of 77%. test 2 has a reliability between 79% and 97% (uncertain due to limited operational data). test 3 has a reliability of 89%. What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.9947 to 0.9992. Point estimate: 0.997.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 tests fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.23 × 0.21 × 0.11 = 0.0053. P(works) = 0.9947.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.23 × 0.03 × 0.11 = 0.0008. P(works) = 0.9992.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
